rulings.Case Title: TANYA PHILLIPS VS. ELEANOR GAUSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Eleanor Gause Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday January 6, 2016, line 15. Defendant Eleanor Gause Motion For Summary Judgment GRANTED. The pleadings define the issues to be considered on a motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff improperly split her causes of action arising out of the same primary right. See CUD-11-639554 and CGC-14-540433. Plaintiff fails to make any allegations, cite any authority, or provide any evidence in support of the cause of action for conversion re: "ownership and right to possession" of funds due from the housing authority to the landlord, therefore Plaintiff's claim is barred by 2-year statute of limitations. Plaintiff's negligence claim is also barred by 2-year statute of limitations; "A landlord and tenant do not generally stand in a fiduciary relationship." Plaintiff fails to allege or provide evidence giving rise to fiduciary relationship = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD C. BAKER VS. CLIMB REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday January 6, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT CLIMB REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC., MELODY HULTGREN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. See Kruse v. Bank of America (1988) 201 Cal.App.3d 354 and Gravillis v. Coldwell Banker (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 761.The facts of this case are distinguishable from Godfrey v. Steinpress (1982) 128 Cal.App.3d 154. = (501/REQ)
